description: Email Service Phase 5 — Observability & Hardening: structured logging via @ortho/logger, health check endpoint, SIGTERM graceful shutdown, Datadog dashboard definition, and API Gateway routing rule for SendGrid webhook

userStories.title: Add @ortho/logger structured logging across all service components
userStories.description: As an operator, I want all Email Service components to emit structured logs with Datadog-compatible fields so that I can correlate events across the distributed system.
acceptanceCriteria: Import @ortho/logger in all route handlers: src/routes/sends.ts, src/routes/campaigns.ts, src/routes/spam-check.ts, src/routes/domains.ts, src/routes/webhooks.ts
Import @ortho/logger in all services: src/services/sendgrid-client.ts, src/services/spam-scanner.ts, src/services/domain-resolver.ts, src/services/webhook-processor.ts
Import @ortho/logger in both workers: src/workers/transactional-send.worker.ts, src/workers/campaign-recipient.worker.ts
Request-entry log in each route handler includes relevant context fields (location_id, job_id, email_id where applicable)
Worker start/completion logs include job_id, email_id or recipient_id, sendgrid_message_id, and attempt count where applicable
All catch/error paths log the full error object at error level
Domain resolver logs cache hits vs. DB misses at debug level
Webhook processor logs per-event routing decision (campaign vs. transactional path) and any dropped events (sendgrid_message_id not found) at warn level
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Health check endpoint GET /health
userStories.description: As the ECS task health check, I need GET /health to verify DB and Redis connectivity so that unhealthy tasks are replaced automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /health performs a DB connectivity check (SELECT 1 via Knex) and a Redis connectivity check (PING via ioredis client)
Returns 200 { "status": "ok", "checks": { "db": true, "redis": true } } when both pass
Returns 503 { "status": "error", "checks": { "db": <bool>, "redis": <bool> } } when either fails, with the failed check set to false
Route is registered in app.ts at GET /health (no auth required)
Unit test: mocked DB and Redis both pass → 200 { status: 'ok' }
Unit test: DB check throws → 503 with db: false
Unit test: Redis PING fails → 503 with redis: false
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SIGTERM graceful shutdown — drain workers and close DB pool
userStories.description: As an ECS operator, I want the Email Service to handle SIGTERM gracefully so that in-flight sends complete before the task exits and no messages are lost.
acceptanceCriteria: src/index.ts registers a process.on('SIGTERM', ...) handler
On SIGTERM: call fastify.close() to stop accepting new HTTP requests
On SIGTERM: call worker.close() on the transactional-send BullMQ worker (waits for in-flight job to finish)
On SIGTERM: call worker.close() on the campaign-recipient BullMQ worker
On SIGTERM: close the Knex DB connection pool after workers drain
On SIGTERM: call process.exit(0) after all resources are closed
Workers are instantiated before the SIGTERM handler is registered so their references are in scope
A comment in index.ts notes ECS stop timeout must be >= 30s to allow campaign-recipient workers to finish
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Datadog dashboard definition for Email Service
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want a committed Datadog dashboard definition so that the Email Service monitoring dashboard can be provisioned from code.
acceptanceCriteria: File created at apps/platform/email/datadog/dashboard.json containing a valid Datadog dashboard API v1 JSON definition
Dashboard title: 'Email Service — Platform'
Widget: BullMQ transactional-send queue depth (metric: bullmq.queue.waiting, queue tag: transactional-send); monitor alert threshold > 1000 for 5 minutes
Widget: BullMQ campaign-recipient queue depth (metric: bullmq.queue.waiting, queue tag: campaign-recipient); monitor alert threshold > 50000 for 10 minutes
Widget: Campaign send rate — emails sent per minute derived from sent_count increments (metric: email_service.campaign.sent_count or equivalent custom metric)
Widget: Webhook processing lag — p95 time from SendGrid event timestamp to processing completion; monitor alert threshold p95 > 30s
Widget: Spam check failure rate — percentage of campaign send jobs blocked by spam gate (status = spam_check_failed / total jobs)
Widget: email.failed dead-letter rate — count of email.failed EventBridge events; alert on any occurrence
dashboard.json is valid JSON (no syntax errors)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: API Gateway routing rule for SendGrid webhook endpoint
userStories.description: As the infrastructure owner, I want the SendGrid webhook endpoint isolated behind an API Gateway routing rule so that only SendGrid traffic reaches POST /webhooks/sendgrid.
acceptanceCriteria: File created at apps/platform/email/infra/api-gateway-sendgrid-rule.ts defining an AWS CDK construct for the API Gateway routing rule
Routing rule matches POST /webhooks/sendgrid path
Routing rule includes SendGrid IP CIDR allowlist (documented comment with reference to SendGrid's published IP ranges)
The construct exports a description string noting this rule is separate from the main Email Service API listener
A comment notes the rule forwards to the same ECS service target group as the main API but is registered as a separate listener rule with higher priority
Typecheck passes
